channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia is investigating reports of crashes plaguing RTX 5090 and 5080 GPUs, with possible driver issues maybe hitting RTX 4000 models too
item.description: Lots of potential workarounds are floating about, including dropping your monitor’s refresh rate – but let’s hope Nvidia can push out a fix swiftly.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OnePlus Open 2 could make the Galaxy Fold 6 look old with invisible crease, according to new teaser
item.description: Another official picture of the Oppo Find N5 – also known as the OnePlus Open 2 – has appeared online.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s order declaring diversity, equity and inclusion policies illegal has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Terry Chea And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4 million for allegedly taking drivers’ tips
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data
item.description: Democrats are turning to inspector general's offices, including those swept up in Trump's firing of several IG's last month, to investigate DOGE's data access. The post Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Data Broker Gravy Analytics Sued for Failing to Protect Personal Data
item.description: Data broker Gravy Analytics is facing a lawsuit after allegedly failing to protect personal data, with 17TB of records stolen from the firm's AWS S3 storage buckets.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump gives vote of confidence in Musk on DOGE
item.description: President Trump on Friday gave a vote of confidence in tech entrepreneur Elon Musk’s work on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), saying that his billionaire ally is doing a “very good job.” “Elon is doing a great job. He’s finding tremendous fraud and corruption and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon's Android TV update hints at new Fire TV devices – maybe at the next-gen AI Alexa event soon
item.description: It looks like Amazon is plowing ahead with new Fire TV devices for Android 14, and we might see them soon.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google Scraps Diversity Hiring Targets Amid Trump Executive Order Pressure
item.description: Google scraps diversity hiring targets amid Trump executive order pressure, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their DEI programs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Messages could soon let you delete messages for other people, and I can't wait to pretend my cringiest jokes never happened
item.description: Google has added a new unsend feature to the most recent unreleased version of Google Messages, which will allow you to delete messages for everybody in a chat rather than only yourself.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Can’t buy an Nvidia RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU due to stock shortages? If you were hoping GeForce Now might provide a good alternative, we’ve got some bad news
item.description: Nvidia GeForce Now goes from bad to worse for sign-ups, with no plans available at all, now – but day passes will return in a couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Like TikTok, lawmakers want to ban DeepSeek from government devices
item.description: A bipartisan duo in the the U.S. House is proposing legislation to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from federal devices, similar to the policy already in place for the popular social media platform TikTok. Reps. Josh Gottheimer, D-N.J., and Darin LaHood, R-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bluesky photo-sharing app Flashes launches in beta
item.description: Flashes, a photo-sharing app that’s linked to X-alternative Bluesky, launched in beta this week before disappearing from the test store due to what its developer said were technical difficulties.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: This startup can measure custom insoles with just an iPhone camera
item.description: The Brannock device—that sliding metal gadget used in shoe stores to measure the dimensions of your feet—was invented 100 years ago this year. But footwear fitting hasn’t really gotten more advanced since, says Dan Cataldi, founder and CEO of custom insole maker Groov.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of rigged votes can skew AI model rankings on Chatbot Arena, study finds
item.description: The generative AI revolution has turned into a global race, with mixtures of models from private companies and open-source initiatives all competing to become the most popular and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Judge blocks Trump plan for federal worker resignations
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk, one of President Donald Trump's most powerful advisers, has orchestrated an unprecedented financial incentive for people to leave their government jobs, promising several months of pay in return for their resignation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Telescopes spy a monster radio jet streaming from a bright and early object in the universe
item.description: The jet of radio waves is the biggest ever detected so early in the history of the universe, astronomers reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Hillary Clinton, Duffy tangle over DOGE involvement in Transportation Department
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton and Department of Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy exchanged criticisms online Wednesday over the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) working with various federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data
item.description: A Treasury official says DOGE team members have “read-only” access to the data from its payment systems as part of an “operational efficiency assessment." The post DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks
item.description: There is no information on who has been hired under DOGE, the authority under which it operates, or how it vets and monitors its staff. The post DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Malicious package found in the Go ecosystem – update
item.description: Update February 6: Google has removed the package and provided the following statement: The module has been removed from both the Go module proxy and GitHub, and we’ve added it to the Go vulnerability database for anyone who thinks they may have been impacted.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia claims near 50% boost in AI storage speed
item.description: Nvidia is touting a near 50% improvement in storage read bandwidth thanks to intelligence in its Spectrum-X Ethernet networking equipment, according to the vendor’s technical blog post.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI-Powered Policing: The Future of Traffic Safety in Kazakhstan
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hacked on social? Here’s what to do next
item.description: Spambots, data thieves, and other bad actors run rampant online — more so now than ever before. Here's what to do if one gets ahold of you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
